Bash(python3 _bmad/scripts/memlog.py append --workspace _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-bingo-2026-07-04 --type decision --by user --text "Win detection: server-side Postgres function/trigger fired on cell-check writes, writes winner\(s\) to Partie table. Bash(python3 _bmad/scripts/memlog.py append --workspace _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-bingo-2026-07-04 --type decision --by coach --text "Guest identity: Supabase anonymous auth, session persisted client-side \(localStorage\), survives reload/reconnect. Binds: FR-8, FR-15. Prevents: hand-rolled guest token/session scheme duplicating what Supabase Auth already provides. Rule: every Joueur \(account or guest\) has a Supabase auth identity \(anonymous or persistent\); no separate guest session mechanism.")

Bash(python3 _bmad/scripts/memlog.py append --workspace _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-bingo-2026-07-04 --type decision --by coach --text "Phrase text propagation: ADOPTED, derived directly from FR-3's tested consequence. Binds: Case/Phrase relationship. Prevents: denormalizing phrase text onto Case at distribution time, which would break live phrase-text corrections \(FR-3\) from reaching already-dealt grids. Rule: Case rows store a phrase_id foreign key + fixed grid position only, never a copy of phrase text; phrase text is always read live via join, so edits propagate automatically through existing realtime subscriptions.")

Bash(python3 _bmad/scripts/memlog.py append --workspace _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-bingo-2026-07-04 --type decision --by coach --text "Realtime mechanism: Supabase Realtime Postgres Changes \(row-level change events\) on Partie and Case tables, not a separate Broadcast channel. Binds: FR-3, FR-10 to FR-15. Prevents: maintaining two parallel realtime mechanisms \(DB state + pub/sub messaging\) for what is fundamentally one authoritative state store. Rule: all live UI updates \(cell checked, phrase corrected, winner declared, game closed, transient toasts\) are derived from Postgres Changes subscriptions on Partie/Case/Grille tables.")
